content.problemStatement:
In this example, we demonstrate how to print character, string, and boolean literals in Java within a structured program.

The `System.out.println` function is used to display different types of literals on the console.
content.outputFormat:
Print exactly three lines in the following order:
A
John
true

sampleTestCases.explanation: The program prints the three required literals, each on its own line.

solution.approach: The task does not require any computation. The program simply prints three constant literals in the required order. To satisfy the platform's convention of handling multiple test cases, we internally set the number of test cases T to 1 and iterate once, printing the three lines during each iteration.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) – constant work regardless of input.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
